Baseline benchmark: gomutant on diffyml (5 gremlins-equivalent mutators)
Date: 2026-04-04
Machine: darwin/arm64
Go: 1.26.1
Workers: 10
Mutants: 792 (772 tested, 20 not covered)
Efficacy: 100%

=== Without per-test coverage map ===
Run 1: 2m39.84s wall
Run 2: 3m51.72s wall (outlier — system load)
Run 3: 2m37.14s wall
Median: 2m39.84s (159.84s)

=== With per-test coverage map (compile-once) ===
Run 1: 2m39.33s wall
Note: -run filter reduces test execution per mutant, but go test -overlay
recompilation dominates (~1-3s per mutant regardless of test count).
Map building adds ~30s overhead for 1193 tests across 2 packages.
Net effect: neutral to slightly negative on small projects.

=== Comparison ===
Gremlins:  275.5s (single run, from golden report)
Gomutant:  ~160s  (median, without test map)
Speedup:   ~42% faster than gremlins

=== Notes ===
Per-test coverage mapping would shine on projects with:
- Large test suites (10k+ tests)
- Slow individual tests (>1s each)
- When combined with mutation switching (compile-once, no recompilation per mutant)

The current bottleneck is go test -overlay triggering recompilation for each mutant.
Mutation switching (Phase 2) would eliminate this by compiling once and toggling
mutants via environment variable at runtime.
